topic: Someone discovers their mother kept every school assignment
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara found the box tucked away in the attic, a dusty cardboard container smelling faintly of lavender and old paper. Inside, nestled among faded photographs, was a stack of folders. Her breath hitched in her throat as she lifted the top one. It was a meticulously organized collection of every assignment she had ever turned in, from kindergarten reports to final thesis drafts. Her fingers trembled as she traced the neat handwriting on a worksheet from sophomore year. A strange, hollow feeling settled in her chest, making her eyes water. "Mom?" she whispered, the sound barely audible in the quiet space.

[story 2]

Mark was clearing out his childhood room, a task he’d been putting off for months. He unearthed a large, labeled bin labeled "Schoolwork." When he opened it, a wave of disbelief washed over him. Hundreds of papers, each one a testament to his academic life, neatly filed and preserved. He stared at the sheer volume, his jaw slackening. He slowly picked up a geometry test, the crisp edges of the paper feeling alien in his hands. A sudden dryness coated his mouth, and he took a deep, unsteady breath, trying to process the impossible sight before him.

[story 3]

When Sarah finally opened the locked wooden chest her grandmother had left her, she expected old jewelry. Instead, she found binders upon binders, each filled with meticulously completed schoolwork spanning decades. Her hands hovered over the contents, hesitant. Slowly, she pulled out a binder from middle school. The weight of the papers felt immense, grounding her at the same time. A small, involuntary gasp escaped her lips. She blinked rapidly, trying to pull the reality of the situation into focus, her gaze darting from one perfectly preserved essay to the next, a tremor running through her arms.
